functional_requirements.requirement: The system allows users to choose between using the service center side or the car owner side.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: A person starts by choosing whether they are using the service center side or the car owner side.

functional_requirements.requirement: Service center users can register or log in if already registered.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they are a service center, they can either register or log in if already registered.

functional_requirements.requirement: After logging in, service center users can enter damaged parts, update parts information, or send expiry-related notifications.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: After logging in, they reach their home area where they can enter damaged parts, update parts information, or send expiry-related notifications.

functional_requirements.requirement: Service center users can log out after finishing any of the above activities.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: After finishing any of these, they can continue using the system or log out.

functional_requirements.requirement: Car owner users can register or log in.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they are a car owner, they can also register or log in.

functional_requirements.requirement: After logging in, car owners can view parts information, request or respond to services, or report a service center.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: After logging in, they reach their home area where they can view parts information, request or respond to services, or report a service center.

functional_requirements.requirement: Car owners can select required parts and make a payment when choosing a service.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they choose a service, they can select the required parts and make a payment, and then continue.

functional_requirements.requirement: Car owners can log out after completing their activities.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: After completing their activities, they can log out, and it ends.

prompt:
You are a requirements engineer.
Extract functional software requirements from the source text.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this schema:
{
  "functional_requirements": [
    {
      "requirement": "string",
      "evidence_quote": "short exact quote from source text"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
1. Include only system capabilities/behaviors.
2. Exclude background context, impact statements, problem motivation, and pure business goals.
3. Keep each requirement atomic and clear.
4. Preserve original meaning. Do not invent functionality.
5. If uncertain, keep the item but keep evidence_quote explicit.

Source text:
<<<
A person starts by choosing whether they are using the service center side or the car owner side .

If they are a service center, they can either register or log in if already registered. After logging in, they reach their home area where they can enter damaged parts, update parts information, or send expiry-related notifications. After finishing any of these, they can continue using the system or log out.

If they are a car owner, they can also register or log in. After logging in, they reach their home area where they can view parts information, request or respond to services, or report a service center.

If they choose a service, they can select the required parts and make a payment, and then continue.

After completing their activities, they can log out, and it ends.
>>>
